mentions.note: Trust trimmed some shares during the 2025 slide on re-up/membership softness and CFO Richard Galante's departure, but Larry Williams's valuation, accumulation, and cycle indicators all flag Costco as a 'screaming buy' into 2026.

Trading at 50x earnings (vs historical high of 47x); re-up rate weak but numbers coming through. Stock bounced on technicals after pantry-stocking discount already priced in. Stay long despite valuation concerns.
Up 3% on ISM-triggered retail buying; benefiting from economic strength signals.
Value retailer making things cheaper than competitors; strong business model; beneficiary of rotation to tangible companies.
Card renewal rates declining, particularly younger consumers; shift to e-commerce over warehouse membership; critical metric is membership re-upsell rate.
Hold for new highs; when gasoline spikes, traditional entry point to buy. Stock rallied; wait for pullback.
MVP (Most Value for Price) brand identified by 100X data. Offers bargain business model with proven value creation. Compounding growth candidate.
Up 12% YTD; high multiples but fortress quality wins in slowdown; good hedge if Fed cuts rates.
Buy in this environment; recovered 50% from January lows but pulled back; defensive play in hard times; very long for trust, like more now.
Down despite rally; retail weakness disappointing.
Two catalysts: cheapest gasoline drives membership card sales where money is made; inflation fighter critical as inflation expected to return.
Nico's portfolio; cheap gas not great for them but high gas drives card signups which is profit driver; rolling out more gasoline stations.
Sells at 49x earnings, never cheap but always terrific; longest-holding stock for trust; sensational fundamentals; would not buy right here given valuation.
Gotten red hot; warehouse club significantly outperforming; preferred long-term choice over BJ's Wholesale Club.

Caller Hope asked long-term entry. Trading at 47x earnings, $974. Cramer: buy some now, hope pullback to 45x. Worst case runs to $1,025, then exit. Buy value incrementally rather than all-in at one level.
